source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/00_기타 작업 서류/240718 - DK 제품별 자료 정리/DK_제품별 자료 정리(pdf)/2. HAD1417MCW/3. 소프트웨어 밸리데이션 문서/Software 62304 checklist_Ver 20200921.pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: Checklist mapping IEC 62304 medical device software lifecycle requirements to supporting documents and applicability by software safety class.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Used to document software lifecycle, risk management, configuration management, maintenance, release, and problem resolution evidence for medical device software.
keyPoints: Software safety classes A, B, and C are assigned according to possible effects on patients, operators, or other people.
Risk management process is required to comply with ISO 14971.
Checklist references supporting document A-TCF-5-046 and sections such as Software Requirements Specification, Hazard Analysis, Software Verification and Validation Test, and Revision History.
SOUP-related checklist items are repeatedly marked not applicable.

entities.name: Common Sense Systems, Inc.
entities.evidence: This document contains confidential information proprietary to Common Sense Systems, Inc.

entities.name: ISO 14971
entities.evidence: The MANUFACTURER shall apply a RISK MANAGEMENT PROCESS complying with ISO 14971.

entities.name: A-TCF-5-046
entities.evidence: Reference Software Lifecycle Process Applicable for Common Sense Systems IEC 62304 Checklist

concepts.name: Software safety classification
concepts.evidence: The MANUFACTURER shall assign to each SOFTWARE SYSTEM a software safety class (A, B or C) according to the possible effects on the patient, operator or other people.

concepts.name: SOUP
concepts.evidence: If a SOFTWARE ITEM is identified as SOUP, the MANUFACTURER has specified functional and performance requirements for the SOUP item that are necessary for its intended use.

evidence.claim: The document is titled Common Sense Systems IEC 62304 Checklist.
The checklist covers IEC 62304 software safety classes A, B, and C.
evidence.evidenceText: The MANUFACTURER shall assign to each SOFTWARE SYSTEM a software safety class (A, B or C) according to the possible effects on the patient, operator or other people.

evidence.claim: Class A means no injury or damage to health is possible.
evidence.evidenceText: Class A: No injury or damage to health is possible

evidence.claim: Class B means non-serious injury is possible.
evidence.evidenceText: Class B: Non-SERIOUS INJURY is possible

evidence.claim: Class C means death or serious injury is possible.
evidence.evidenceText: Class C: Death or SERIOUS INJURY is possible

evidence.claim: The manufacturer shall apply a risk management process complying with ISO 14971.
evidence.evidenceText: The MANUFACTURER shall apply a RISK MANAGEMENT PROCESS complying with ISO 14971.

evidence.claim: Software requirements must include regulatory requirements as appropriate to the medical device software.
evidence.evidenceText: As appropriate to the MEDICAL DEVICE SOFTWARE, the MANUFACTURER has included regulatory requirements in the software requirements.

evidence.claim: Software verification must be completed and evaluated before software release.
evidence.evidenceText: The MANUFACTURER has ensured that software VERIFICATION was completed and the results EVALUATED before the software was released.

evidence.claim: Known residual anomalies must be evaluated to ensure they do not contribute to unacceptable risk.
evidence.evidenceText: The MANUFACTURER has ensured that all known residual ANOMALIES were EVALUATED to ensure that they did not contribute to an unacceptable risk.

evidence.claim: The software maintenance plan addresses post-release feedback handling.
evidence.evidenceText: The software maintenance plan addresses procedures for receiving, documenting, evaluating, resolving and tracking feedback arising after release of the MEDICAL DEVICE SOFTWARE.

reviewFlags.reason: Document date is not explicitly present; filename contains Ver 20200921 but no confirmed document date.
Authority is ambiguous: source identifies Common Sense Systems, Inc. as proprietary owner, but authoritySignal is general and no regulator or jurisdiction is stated.
Product model appears only in source metadata productSignal, not in the extracted document text.
OCR/text quality issues are present, including misspellings such as PROCESESS and Software Lift Cycle.
